name: Melee Attack
description: A close-range attack that uses a hitbox, weapon arc, or contact trace near the attacker.
design_notes: Melee attacks are built from timing, reach, facing, collision, recovery, and hit reaction. A useful melee entry should define startup, active frames, cancel rules, target filtering, and how the game communicates range. Weapon feel often comes from hit stop, sound, camera response, and enemy reaction as much as damage numbers.
design_purpose: Give the player a direct close-range way to apply pressure or defeat enemies.
Create spacing decisions through reach, startup, recovery, and hit stun.
Support combat identity through weapon arcs, combo links, and target reactions.
player_fantasy: The player feels physically connected to the fight, reading distance and timing to land decisive hits.

parameters.name: melee_startup_frames
parameters.description: Frames before the attack can hit.

parameters.name: melee_active_frames
parameters.description: Frames during which the hitbox or trace can connect.

parameters.name: melee_recovery_frames
parameters.description: Frames before the attacker can take the next full action.

parameters.name: hitbox_forward_extent
parameters.description: Reach of the attack in front of the actor.

parameters.name: hit_stop_frames_on_connect
parameters.description: Brief freeze applied on hit for impact readability.

edge_cases: Fast movement during active frames should not let the hitbox skip through small targets.
The same swing must define whether it can hit one target once, many targets once, or the same target repeatedly.
Attacking near walls should handle weapon clipping and blocked arcs consistently.
Animation cancel, parry, dodge, and hit stun must agree on when the attack is interruptible.
common_bugs: Damage is applied every frame of overlap because hit targets are not tracked per attack instance.
Hitboxes spawn from animated bones that update after physics, causing one-frame visual mismatch.
Attack range differs between left and right facing due to unmirrored offsets.
Combo input is accepted during hit stop but discarded when the simulation resumes.
balancing_notes: Long reach and low recovery make melee safe; slower recovery or commitment preserves enemy counterplay.
Hit stun, knockback, and cancel windows should be balanced together because they determine whether pressure loops are possible.
accessibility_notes: Use clear hit confirmation through impact sound, hit flash, controller vibration, or hit markers.
Avoid requiring hidden animation-frame knowledge for basic attacks; reserve tight cancels for advanced mastery.

implementation_notes.unity: Model startup, active, recovery, and cancel windows as data, then drive hitboxes, animation events, and damage application from one combat state source. For Melee Attack, validate this path with parameters such as melee_startup_frames and melee_active_frames.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D hit volumes with frame-window state on the actor; avoid placing damage authority inside animation-only callbacks. For Melee Attack, validate this path with parameters such as melee_startup_frames and melee_active_frames.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement the action as a Gameplay Ability or Actor Component with montage notifies for windows and authoritative damage on the server when networked. For Melee Attack, validate this path with parameters such as melee_startup_frames and melee_active_frames.
implementation_notes.web: Update combat windows in the fixed simulation step, evaluate hurtbox overlap from cached transforms, and emit presentation events separately from damage state. For Melee Attack, validate this path with parameters such as melee_startup_frames and melee_active_frames.
